rulings.Case Title: DAVID MARTINEZ ET AL VS. CERTAINTEED CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, November 8, 2017 in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Underground Construction Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent David Martinez, Sr. was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, defendant did not demonstrate that plaintiff David Martinez, Jr. does not possess knowledge that defendant's employees cut and/or beveled asbestos cement pipe in decedent's presence. The excerpts of David Martinez, Jr.'s deposition testimony submitted with defendant's moving papers address only one specific job site. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Additionally, defendant failed to sustain its burden of negating an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to the declaration of Kyle Dotson are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/CML)
